title: Live Model Comparison — Code Explanation
prompt: In 2–3 sentences, what is a closure in JavaScript and why is it useful?
guidelines: Both responses are generated live via Hugging Face Inference API — you can edit the prompt before sending.
Select two different models from the dropdowns to compare their outputs.
Compare clarity, technical accuracy, and conciseness.

inference.system: You are a concise programming tutor. Answer in markdown. Keep under 150 words.

dimensions.name: Helpfulness
dimensions.description: Does it answer the question clearly and completely?

dimensions.name: Conciseness
dimensions.description: Is the answer appropriately brief without losing key details?

title: Live Model Comparison — Bug Diagnosis
prompt:
This Python code has a bug. Find it and explain the fix:

def merge_dicts(a, b, result={}):
    result.update(a)
    result.update(b)
    return result
guidelines: Both responses are generated live — you can edit the prompt or try your own.
Compare how well each model identifies the mutable default argument bug.
Check whether the explanation would help a junior developer understand the issue.

inference.system: You are an expert code reviewer. Identify bugs precisely. Use markdown with code blocks.

dimensions.name: Bug Identification
dimensions.description: Does it correctly identify the mutable default argument issue?

dimensions.name: Explanation Quality
dimensions.description: Is the explanation clear enough for a junior developer?

dimensions.name: Fix Correctness
dimensions.description: Is the proposed fix correct and idiomatic?
